frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Divya: I’m not saying anyone should be forced, but honestly after marriage these small things matter no? At least a mangalsutra or a little sindoor shows respect for the relationship and our culture. Otherwise sometimes it feels like people want the marriage without accepting any of its identity.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it frames visible marital markers as a natural, respectful duty of a married woman rather than control. It encodes tradition framing and judgment of women who do not wear mangalsutra or sindoor as not fully accepting marriage.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Divya. Age 35. A senior software engineer in Bengaluru. You are married with one child. You are venting to your husband at home after dinner. Your skip level manager pulled you into a meeting and told you that if you plan to have another child you should think carefully about whether this is the right time to take on a high visibility project. You are telling your husband what was said to you.
